name: German Butterball
image_alt: Golden-skinned German Butterball potatoes with yellow flesh
description: A beloved heirloom potato with golden skin and rich, buttery yellow flesh. Versatile enough for any cooking method and forgiving enough for beginner growers. Produces heavy yields of medium-to-large tubers with a creamy texture that needs no embellishment.
history: A German heirloom brought to America by European immigrants, German Butterball has been grown continuously in American kitchen gardens for well over a century. Its name is entirely descriptive — the flavor genuinely tastes of butter even without adding any.
seasonal_quirks: A late-season potato that stores exceptionally well through winter. Hill soil around stems as vines grow to maximize yield. Flavor deepens in storage — potatoes dug in fall often taste better by midwinter.
harvest_cues: Wait until vines die back completely for full-size tubers. For new potatoes, harvest 2–3 weeks after flowering. Dig carefully — skins are tender at harvest and cure for 1–2 weeks in a cool dark place before storage.
variety_notes: One of the best all-purpose heirloom potatoes available. Performs well roasted, mashed, boiled, or fried. The yellow flesh holds its color when cooked. Less prone to hollow heart than many large-tuber varieties.
preservation: Cure and store in cool, dark, humid conditions — 38–40°F ideal. Stores 4–6 months. Do not refrigerate unwashed potatoes.
